member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter
member.summary: Various blit (texture copy) utilities for the Scriptable Render Pipelines.
member.remarks:
The Blitter class works on textures and targets identified with , but most importantly with . This enables copying to / from textures managed by the .
To use the Blitter functionality in the context of custom Scriptable Render Pipelines, you must first create a blit shader that implements various passes covering the blit variants. To facilitate this, you can create a modified version of the Universal Render Pipeline CoreBlit.shader as displayed in the documentation of the method.
Prior to using the Blitter, you must call once. When the render pipeline is to be disposed, you must call the method to dispose of resources created by the Blitter.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.Initialize(UnityEngine.Shader,UnityEngine.Shader)
member.summary: Initializes the Blitter resources. This must be called once before any use.
member.param: The shader to use when using the blitting / copying methods which operate only on color.
The shader to use when using the BlitColorAndDepth methods which operate on both color and depth.
member.remarks:
Shaders sent to the blitPS parameter should support multiple passes with the corresponding name:
Nearest
Bilinear
NearestQuad
BilinearQuad
NearestQuadPadding
BilinearQuadPadding
NearestQuadPaddingRepeat
BilinearQuadPaddingRepeat
BilinearQuadPaddingOctahedral
NearestQuadPaddingAlphaBlend
BilinearQuadPaddingAlphaBlend
NearestQuadPaddingAlphaBlendRepeat
BilinearQuadPaddingAlphaBlendRepeat
BilinearQuadPaddingAlphaBlendOctahedral
CubeToOctahedral
CubeToOctahedralLuminance
CubeToOctahedralAlpha
CubeToOctahedralRed
BilinearQuadLuminance
BilinearQuadAlpha
BilinearQuadRed
NearestCubeToOctahedralPadding
BilinearCubeToOctahedralPadding
Basic vertex and fragment shader functions are available in Packages/com.unity.render-pipelines.core/Runtime/Utilities/Blit.hlsl for each of these pass types. Similarly, the shaders for the blitColorAndDepthPS parameter should support two passes with these names:
ColorOnly
ColorAndDepth

example.para: Blit color shader for URP which implements all the above passes with a user defined FragmentURPBlit fragment function to support debug passes and color space conversion.
Blit color and depth shader for URP.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.Cleanup
member.summary: Releases all the internal Blitter resources. Must be called when the Blitter object is to be disposed.

member.summary: Returns the default blit material constructed from the blit shader passed as the first argument of the method.
member.param: Dimension of the texture to blit, either 2D or 2D Array.
Blit only a single slice of the array if applicable.
member.returns: The default blit material for the specified arguments.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.CopyTexture(UnityEngine.Rendering.RasterCommandBuffer,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Copies a texture to another texture using framebuffer fetch.
member.param: Use the MSAA variant of the copy shader (otherwise single sample is used).

Disable the special handling when XR is active where the source and destination are considered array textures with a slice for each eye. Setting this to true will consider source and destination as regular 2D textures. When XR is disabled, textures are always 2D so forcing them to 2D has no impact.

member.summary: Blits a RTHandle texture.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitTexture(UnityEngine.Rendering.RasterCommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Rendering.RTHandle,UnityEngine.Vector4,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds in a a command to copy an XR compatible texture identified by its into the currently bound render target's color buffer.
member.remarks:
Copying is performed using the blit shader passed as the first argument of the method.
This overload is meant for textures and render targets which depend on XR output modes by proper handling, when necessary, of left / right eye data copying. This generally correspond to textures which represent full screen
The scaleBias parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets. The operation will always write to the full destination render target rectangle.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
RTHandle of the source texture to copy from.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.
Mip level of the source texture to copy from.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitTexture(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Rendering.RTHandle,UnityEngine.Vector4,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds in a a command to copy an XR compatible texture identified by its into the currently bound render target's color buffer.
member.remarks:
Copying is performed using the blit shader passed as the first argument of the method.
This overload is meant for textures and render targets which depend on XR output modes by proper handling, when necessary, of left / right eye data copying. This generally correspond to textures which represent full screen data that may differ between eyes.
The scaleBias parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets. The operation will always write to the full destination render target rectangle.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
RTHandle of the source texture to copy from.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.
Mip level of the source texture to copy from.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitColorAndDepth(UnityEngine.Rendering.RasterCommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Texture,UnityEngine.RenderTexture,UnityEngine.Vector4,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds in a a command to copy two XR compatible color and depth textures into the currently bound render target's respective color and depth buffer.
member.remarks:
Although the depth render texture can be passed as a parameter, the copying of the depth information is optional and must be enabled with the blitDepth parameter. The copying is done using the blitColorAndDepth shader passed as the second argument of the method.
This overload is meant for textures and render targets which depend on XR output modes by proper handling, when necessary, of left / right eye data copying. This generally corresponds to textures which represent full screen data that may differ between eyes.
The scaleBias parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets. The operation will always write to the full destination render target rectangle.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
Source color texture to copy from.
Source depth render texture to copy from.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.
Mip level of the source texture to copy from.
Enable copying of the source depth texture.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitColorAndDepth(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Texture,UnityEngine.RenderTexture,UnityEngine.Vector4,System.Single,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds in a a command to copy two XR compatible color and depth textures into the currently bound render target's respective color and depth buffer.
member.remarks:
Although the depth render texture can be passed as a parameter, the copying of the depth information is optional and must be enabled with the blitDepth parameter. The copying is done using the blitColorAndDepth shader passed as the second argument of the method.
This overload is meant for textures and render targets which depend on XR output modes by proper handling, when necessary, of left / right eye data copying. This generally corresponds to textures which represent full screen data that may differ between eyes.
The scaleBias parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets. The operation will always write to the full destination render target rectangle.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
Source color texture to copy from.
Source depth render texture to copy from.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.
Mip level of the source texture to copy from.
Enable copying of the source depth texture.

The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets. Mip level of the source texture to copy from.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitTexture(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Vector4,UnityEngine.Material,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds in a a command to draw a full screen quad, using a user material and specific shader pass.
member.remarks:
This method gives you freedom on how to write your blit shader by just taking a material, assumed to be properly configured with input textures already bound to the material. In this method, the "_BlitScaleBias" shader property will be set on the material to the scaleBias parameter, prior to the draw.
The scaleBias parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets. The operation will always write to the full destination render target rectangle.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.

member.summary: Adds in a a command to copy an XR compatible texture onto a portion of the current render target.
member.remarks:
The scaleBiasTex parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets.
Similarly, the scaleBiasRT parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the render target to write to by manipulating the destination quad coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the coordinate offsets.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
The source texture to copy from.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitQuadWithPadding(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Texture,UnityEngine.Vector2,UnityEngine.Vector4,UnityEngine.Vector4,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds in a a command to copy an XR compatible texture onto a portion of the current render target with support for padding on the destination rect.
member.remarks:
![Diagram of the padding parameters](../manual/images/Blitter_BlitQuadWithPadding.svg)
Diagram detailing the use of the padding, textureSize, scaleBiasTex and scaleBiasRT.
The source rect is copied to the destination rect along with extra padding pixels taken from the source texture using the texture's . Both source rect pixels and padding pixels are copied inside the destination rect.
The scaleBiasTex parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets.
Similarly, the scaleBiasRT parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the render target to write to by manipulating the destination quad coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the coordinate offsets.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
The source texture to copy from.
Scale and bias for sampling the source texture.
Scale and bias for the destination quad.
Mip level of the source texture to sample.
Padding in pixels to add in the destination rect. This is the total padding on an axis so to have N pixels added to the left, and N pixels to the right, paddingInPixels should be set to 2N.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Blitter.BlitQuadWithPaddingMultiply(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Texture,UnityEngine.Vector2,UnityEngine.Vector4,UnityEngine.Vector4,System.Int32,System.Boolean,System.Int32)

member.remarks:
The source rect is blended to the destination rect with a multiplicative blend, along with extra padding pixels taken from the source texture using the texture's . Both source rect pixels and padding pixels are blitted inside the destination rect. See for a diagram of how padding is applied.
The scaleBiasTex parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the source texture to copy by manipulating the source texture coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to these texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the texture coordinate offsets.
Similarly, the scaleBiasRT parameter controls the rectangle of pixels in the render target to write to by manipulating the destination quad coordinates. The X and Y coordinates store the scaling factor to apply to texture coordinates, while the Z and W coordinates store the coordinate offsets.
member.param: Command Buffer used for recording the action.
The source texture to copy from.

Mip level of the source texture to sample.
Padding in pixels to add in the destination rect. This is the total padding on an axis so to have N pixels added to the left, and N pixels to the right, paddingInPixels should be set to 2N.

member.summary: The maximum resolution percentage that dynamic resolution can reach.

member.summary: The default of upscaling filter used. It can be overridden via the API DynamicResolutionHandler.SetUpscaleFilter

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.forceResolution
member.summary: Select whether dynamic resolution system will force a specific resolution percentage.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.forcedPercentage
member.summary: The resolution percentage forced in case forceResolution is set to true.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.lowResTransparencyMinimumThreshold
member.summary: The minimum percentage threshold allowed to clamp low resolution transparency. When the resolution percentage falls below this threshold, HDRP will clamp the low resolution to this percentage.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.rayTracingHalfResThreshold
member.summary: The minimum percentage threshold allowed to render ray tracing effects at half resolution. When the resolution percentage falls below this threshold, HDRP will render ray tracing effects at full resolution.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.lowResSSGIMinimumThreshold
member.summary: The minimum percentage threshold allowed to clamp low resolution for SSGI (Screen Space Global Illumination). When the resolution percentage falls below this threshold, HDRP will clamp the low resolution to this percentage.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.lowResVolumetricCloudsMinimumThreshold
member.summary: The minimum percentage threshold allowed to clamp tracing resolution for Volumetric Clouds. When the resolution percentage falls below this threshold, HDRP will trace the Volumetric Clouds in half res.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.GlobalDynamicResolutionSettings.enableDLSS
member.summary: Obsolete, used only for data migration. Use the advancedUpscalersByPriority list instead to add the proper supported advanced upscaler by priority.

member.summary: Noise to be applied to the sampling position. It can hide seams issues between subdivision levels, but introduces noise.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.ProbeVolumesOptions.animateSamplingNoise
member.summary: Whether to animate the noise when TAA is enabled, smoothing potentially out the noise pattern introduced.

member.summary: Method used to reduce leaks.

member.summary: Global probe volumes weight. Allows for fading out probe volumes influence falling back to ambient probe.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.ProbeVolumesOptions.skyOcclusionIntensityMultiplier
member.summary: Multiplier applied on the sky lighting when using sky occlusion.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.ProbeVolumesOptions.worldOffset
member.summary: Offset applied at runtime to probe positions in world space.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ResourceReloader
member.summary: The resources that need to be reloaded in Editor can live in Runtime. The reload call should only be done in Editor context though but it could be called from runtime entities.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ResourceReloader.TryReloadAllNullIn(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Looks for resources in the given object and reload the ones that are missing or broken, using the to find the paths. This version returns a null value without throwing an error if the issue is AssetDatabase being not ready. But in this case the assetDatabaseNotReady result will be true.
member.param: The base path for the package
member.returns: - 1 hasChange: True if something have been reloaded. - 2 assetDatabaseNotReady: True if the issue preventing loading is due to state of AssetDatabase

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ResourceReloader.ReloadAllNullIn(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Looks for resources in the given object and reload the ones that are missing or broken, using the to find the paths. See for an example.
member.param: The base path for the package
member.returns: True if something have been reloaded.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ResourceReloader.EnsureResources``2(System.Boolean,``0@,System.String,System.Func{``1,System.Boolean},``1)
member.summary: Ensures that all resources in a container has been loaded
member.param: Set to true to force all resources to be reloaded even if they are loaded already
The resource container with the resulting loaded resources
The asset path to load the resource container from
Function to test if the resource container is present in a RenderPipelineGlobalSettings
RenderPipelineGlobalSettings to be passed to checker to test of the resource container is already loaded

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.SHCoefficients
member.summary:
Contains spherical harmonic coefficients used for lighting representation in the format expected by DOTS_INSTANCING_ON shaders.
The size of the struct is padded to a power of two so arrays of such structs can be efficiently indexed in shaders.

member.summary: Intensity

member.summary: Distance used to scale the Distance Attenuation Curve

member.summary: Distance used to scale the Scale Attenuation Curve

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.distanceAttenuationCurve
member.summary: Attenuation by distance

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.scaleByDistanceCurve
member.summary: Scale by distance, use the same distance as distanceAttenuationCurve

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.attenuationByLightShape
member.summary: If component attached to a light, attenuation the lens flare per light type

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.radialScreenAttenuationCurve
member.summary: Attenuation used radially, which allow for instance to enable flare only on the edge of the screen

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.useOcclusion
member.summary: Enable Occlusion feature

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.useBackgroundCloudOcclusion
member.summary: Enable Occlusion using Background Cloud (for instance: CloudLayer) Please use useFogOpacityOcclusion instead.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.environmentOcclusion
member.summary: Enable occlusion from environment effects supported by the render pipeline. This may include opacity from volumetric clouds, background clouds, fog and water.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.useWaterOcclusion
member.summary: Enable Occlusion with Water

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.occlusionRadius
member.summary: Radius around the light used to occlude the flare (value in world space)

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.sampleCount
member.summary: Random Samples Count used inside the disk with 'occlusionRadius'

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.occlusionOffset
member.summary: Z Occlusion Offset allow us to offset the plane where the disc of occlusion is place (closer to camera), value on world space. Useful for instance to sample occlusion outside a light bulb if we place a flare inside the light bulb

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.scale
member.summary: Global Scale

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.allowOffScreen
member.summary: If allowOffScreen is true then If the lens flare is outside the screen we still emit the flare on screen

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.volumetricCloudOcclusion
member.summary: If volumetricCloudOcclusion is true then use the volumetric cloud (on HDRP only) for the occlusion Please use useFogOpacityOcclusion instead.

members.member: Our default celestial body will have an angular radius of 3.3 degrees. This is an arbitrary number, but must be kept constant so the occlusion radius for direct lights is consistent regardless of near / far clip plane configuration.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.occlusionRemapCurve
member.summary: OcclusionRemapCurve allow the occlusion [from 0 to 1] to be remap with any desired shape.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.lightOverride
member.summary: Light override, change the light which influences the flares including "modulate by light color" and "Attenuation By Light Shape" but not the position.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.celestialProjectedOcclusionRadius(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Retrieves the projected occlusion radius from a particular celestial in the infinity plane with an angular radius. This is used for directional lights which require to have consistent occlusion radius regardless of the near/farplane configuration.
member.param: The camera utilized to calculate the occlusion radius
member.returns: The value, in world units, of the occlusion angular radius.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.OnEnable
member.summary: Add or remove the lens flare to the queue of PostProcess

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.OnDisable
member.summary: Remove the lens flare from the queue of PostProcess

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareComponentSRP.OnValidate
member.summary: Add or remove the lens flare from the queue of PostProcess

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentMenu
member.summary: This attribute is used to set up a path in the Add Override popup menu in Unity's Volume system. It allows you to organize and categorize your Volume components into submenus for easier access and management within the editor.
member.remarks: Specify the name of the menu entry, and use slashes ("/") to create hierarchical submenus in the popup. This is useful for organizing large or complex sets of Volume components. To further filter the menu entries based on the active Render Pipeline, you can combine this attribute with the attribute. This enables conditional display of Volume components depending on the Render Pipeline being used in the project.

example.code: [VolumeComponentMenu("MyVolumeCategory/LightingEffects")] public class CustomLightingVolume : VolumeComponent { ... }

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentMenu.menu
member.summary: The name of the entry in the override list. You can use slashes to create sub-menus.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentMenu.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance.
member.param: The name of the entry in the override list. You can use slashes to create sub-menus.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentMenuForRenderPipeline
member.summary: This attribute allows you to add commands to the Add Override popup menu on Volumes, while also specifying the render pipeline(s) for which the command will be supported.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentMenuForRenderPipeline.pipelineTypes
member.summary: The list of pipeline types that the target class supports.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentMenuForRenderPipeline.#ctor(System.String,System.Type[])
member.summary: Creates a new instance.
member.param: The name of the entry in the override list. You can use slashes to create sub-menus.
The list of pipeline types that the target class supports.
member.exception: Thrown when the pipelineTypes is null or the types do not inherit from .

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeComponentDeprecated
member.summary: This attribute prevents the component from being included in the list of available overrides in the Volume Inspector via the Add Override button.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.RTHandle

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.RTHandle.SetCustomHandleProperties(UnityEngine.Rendering.RTHandleProperties@)
member.summary: By default, rtHandleProperties gets the global state of scalers against the global reference mode. This method lets the current RTHandle use a local custom RTHandleProperties. This function is being used by scalers such as TAAU and DLSS, which require to have a different resolution for color (independent of the RTHandleSystem).

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.KeyframeUtility
member.summary: A helper function for interpolating AnimationCurves together. In general, curves can not be directly blended because they will have keypoints at different places. InterpAnimationCurve traverses through the keypoints. If both curves have a keypoint at the same time, they keypoints are trivially lerped together. However if one curve has a keypoint at a time that is missing in the other curve (which is the most common case), InterpAnimationCurve calculates a synthetic keypoint at that time based on value and derivative, and interpolates the resulting keys. Note that this function should only be called by internal rendering code. It creates a small pool of animation curves and reuses them to avoid creating garbage. The number of curves needed is quite small, since curves only need to be used when interpolating multiple volumes together with different curve parameters. The underlying interp function isn't allowed to fail, so in the case where we run out of memory we fall back to returning a single keyframe.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.KeyframeUtility.ResetAnimationCurve(UnityEngine.AnimationCurve)
member.summary: Helper function to remove all control points for an animation curve. Since animation curves are reused in a pool, this function clears existing keys so the curve is ready for reuse.

members.member: In an animation curve, the inTangent and outTangent don't match the edge of the curve. For example, the first key might have inTangent=3.0f but the actual incoming tangent is 0.0 because the curve is clamped outside the time domain. So this helper fetches a key, but zeroes out the inTangent of the first key and the outTangent of the last key.

lhsIndex and rhsIndex are the indices in the keys array. The lhsIndex/rhsIndex may be -1, in which it creates a synthetic first key at startTime, or beyond the length of the array, in which case it creates a synthetic key at endTime.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.KeyframeUtility.InterpAnimationCurve(UnityEngine.AnimationCurve@,UnityEngine.AnimationCurve,System.Single)
member.summary: Interpolates two AnimationCurves. Since both curves likely have control points at different places in the curve, this method will create a new curve from the union of times between both curves. However, to avoid creating garbage, this function will always replace the keys of lhsAndResultCurve with the final result, and return lhsAndResultCurve.
member.param: The start value. Additionaly, this instance will be reused and returned as the result.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ReloadGroupAttribute
member.summary: Attribute specifying that fields of this type should be inspected in depth by the . If the associated class instance is null, the system attempts to recreate it using its default constructor.
member.remarks: Make sure classes using it have a default constructor!

example.para: This shows how to use the attribute in the expected scenario. This is particularly useful for content creators. Adding a new field to a class that defines an asset results in null values for existing instances missing the field in their serialized data. Therefore, when a new field is added, a system for reloading null values may be necessary.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.UnsafeGenericPool`1
member.summary: Generic pool without collection checks. This class is an alternative for the GenericPool for object that allocate memory when they are being compared. It is the case for the CullingResult class from Unity, and because of this in HDRP HDCullingResults generates garbage whenever we use ==, .Equals or ReferenceEquals. This pool doesn't do any of these comparison because we don't check if the stack already contains the element before releasing it.
member.typeparam: Type of the objects in the pull.

member.summary: Get a new object.
member.returns: A new object from the pool.

member.summary: Get a new PooledObject
member.returns: A new PooledObject.

member.summary: Release an object to the pool.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ContextItem
member.summary: This is needed to add the data to ContextContainer and will control how the data are removed when calling Dispose on the ContextContainer.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ContextItem.Reset
member.summary: Resets the object so it can be used as a new instance next time it is created. To avoid memory allocations and generating garbage, the system reuses objects. This function should clear the object so it can be reused without leaking any information (e.g. pointers to objects that will no longer be valid to access). So it is important the implementation carefully clears all relevant members. Note that this is different from a Dispose or Destructor as the object in not freed but reset. This can be useful when havin large sub-allocated objects like arrays or lists which can be cleared and reused without re-allocating.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeParameter
member.summary:
The base class for all parameter types stored in a .
This class serves as a base for different parameter types that are used within the Volume system, such as those controlling post-processing effects, lighting settings, and other volume-related parameters.
The class implements the interface, enabling cloning of the parameter's values for later use or manipulation.
member.remarks:
The class is an abstract base class, and cannot be instantiated directly. Derived classes like represent specific parameter types, such as float, vector, or color values, and can be used to configure individual settings in a Volume component.
It is important to note that this class is designed to allow various parameter types to be stored and managed collectively. This mechanism enables developers to create complex and configurable Volume components that can be customized with different parameter types (e.g., sliders for float values, color pickers for color values, etc.).

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeParameter.k_DebuggerDisplay
member.summary: A beautified string for debugger output. This is set on a DebuggerDisplay on every parameter types.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeParameter.m_OverrideState
member.summary: The current override state for this parameter. The Volume system considers overriden parameters for blending, and ignores non-overriden ones.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeParameter.overrideState
member.summary: The current override state for this parameter. The Volume system considers overriden parameters for blending, and ignores non-overriden ones. It is also used in the VolumeStack to determine whether a given parameter has been overridden and thus needs to be reset to its default state in the next update.
member.remarks: You can override this property to define custom behaviors when the override state changes.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.AnimationCurveParameter.Interp(UnityEngine.AnimationCurve,UnityEngine.AnimationCurve,System.Single)
member.summary: Interpolates between two AnimationCurve values. Note that it will overwrite the values in lhsCurve, whereas rhsCurve data will be unchanged. Thus, it is legal to call it as: stateParam.Interp(stateParam, toParam, interpFactor); However, It should NOT be called when the lhsCurve parameter needs to be preserved. But the current framework modifies it anyway in VolumeComponent.Override for all types of VolumeParameters

member.summary: Disable all log for Shader Variant

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ShaderStrippingSetting
member.summary: This is a Graphics Settings container for settings related to shader stripping for all scriptable render pipelines.
member.remarks: To change those settings, go to Editor > Project Settings in the Graphics tab. Changing this through the API is only allowed in the Editor. In the Player, this raises an error.

example.para: Here is an example of how to check if your project strips variant shaders when building a Player using URP.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.ShaderStrippingSetting.version
member.summary: Current version of the settings container. Used only for project upgrades.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.ShaderStrippingSetting.exportShaderVariants
member.summary: Controls whether to output shader variant information to a file.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.ShaderStrippingSetting.shaderVariantLogLevel
member.summary: Controls the level of logging of shader variant information outputted during the build process. Information appears in the Unity Console when the build finishes.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.ShaderStrippingSetting.stripRuntimeDebugShaders
member.summary: When enabled, all debug display shader variants are removed when you build for the Unity Player. This decreases build time, but prevents the use of most Rendering Debugger features in Player builds.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.HDROutputUtils
member.summary: Utility class for outputting to an HDR display.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.HDROutputUtils.Operation
member.summary: HDR color operations that the shader applies.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.HDROutputUtils.Operation.None
member.summary: Do not perform operations specific to HDR output.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.HDROutputUtils.Operation.ColorConversion
member.summary: Convert colors to the color space of the HDR display.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP
member.summary: Provides methods for implementing lens flares in a render pipeline.
member.remarks:
The High Definition Render Pipeline (HDRP) and Universal Render Pipeline (URP) use this class for their lens flare implementation. The class supports both screen space lens flares and quad-based lens flares. You must call the methods of `LensFlareCommonSRP` at several places inside the Scriptable Render Pipeline (SRP). At minimum, you must call the method. You can use any of these methods in a `Monobehaviour` script.
.
Note that only one `LensFlareCommonSRP` can be alive at any point. To call members of this class, use the property.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.LensFlareCompInfo
member.summary: Class describing internal information stored to describe a shown LensFlare

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.LensFlareCompInfo.index
member.summary: Index used to compute Occlusion in a fixed order

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.LensFlareCompInfo.comp
member.summary: Component used

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.maxLensFlareWithOcclusion
member.summary: Defines how many lens flare with occlusion are supported in the view at any time.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.maxLensFlareWithOcclusionTemporalSample
member.summary: With TAA Occlusion jitter depth, thought frame on HDRP. So we do a "unanimity vote" for occlusion thought 'maxLensFlareWithOcclusionTemporalSample' frame Important to keep this value maximum of 8 If this value change that could implies an implementation modification on: com.unity.render-pipelines.high-definition/Runtime/PostProcessing/Shaders/LensFlareMergeOcclusionDataDriven.compute

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.mergeNeeded
member.summary: Set to 1 to enable temporal sample merge. Set to 0 to disable temporal sample merge (must support 16 bit textures, and the occlusion merge must be written in the last texel (vertical) of the lens flare texture.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.occlusionRT
member.summary: occlusion texture either provided or created automatically by the SRP for lens flare. Texture width is the max number of lens flares that have occlusion (x axis the lens flare index). y axis is the number of samples (maxLensFlareWithOcclusionTemporalSample) plus the number of merge results. Merge results must be done by the SRP and stored in the [(lens flareIndex), (maxLensFlareWithOcclusionTemporalSample + 1)] coordinate. Note: It's not supported on OpenGL3 and OpenGLCore

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.LensFlareCommonSRP.IsOcclusionRTCompatible
member.summary: Check if we can create OcclusionRT texture to be used as render target
member.returns: Returns true if a supported format is found

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray
member.summary: IBitArray interface.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.capacity
member.summary: Gets the capacity of this BitArray. This is the number of bits that are usable.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.allFalse
member.summary: Return `true` if all the bits of this BitArray are set to 0. Returns `false` otherwise.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.allTrue
member.summary: Return `true` if all the bits of this BitArray are set to 1. Returns `false` otherwise.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.Item(System.UInt32)
member.summary: An indexer that allows access to the bit at a given index. This provides both read and write access.
member.value: State of the bit at the provided index.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.humanizedData
member.summary: Writes the bits in the array in a human-readable form. This is as a string of 0s and 1s packed by 8 bits. This is useful for debugging.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.BitAnd(UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray)
member.summary: Perform an AND bitwise operation between this BitArray and the one you pass into the function and return the result. Both BitArrays must have the same capacity. This will not change current BitArray values.
member.param: BitArray with which to the And operation.
member.returns: The resulting bit array.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.BitOr(UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray)
member.summary: Perform an OR bitwise operation between this BitArray and the one you pass into the function and return the result. Both BitArrays must have the same capacity. This will not change current BitArray values.
member.param: BitArray with which to the Or operation.
member.returns: The resulting bit array.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.IBitArray.BitNot
member.summary: Return the BitArray with every bit inverted.
member.returns: The resulting bit array.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.IPerFrameHistoryAccessTracker
member.summary: User API to request access for an instance of the user history type. Tracks the history types that were requested by the render pipeline features on this frame. Requested history types are then made available for the future frames. Request is active for one frame only and a new request should be made every frame. Types that were not requested are eventually reset and GPU resources released.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.IPerFrameHistoryAccessTracker.RequestAccess``1
member.summary: Mark a certain history texture type (class) as a requirement for next frame. Note: Requesting a history doesn't mean it will be actually available. E.g. The first frame doesn't have any history data available at all.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryWriteAccess
member.summary: User API to get history write access for a user history type instance. Write access is valid and available after the history type has been requested. Otherwise a null is returned. Typically called by the history type producer render pass in the render pipeline.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryWriteAccess.IsAccessRequested``1
member.summary: Check if a type has been requested and should be written this frame.
member.returns: True if a type has been requested earlier. False otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryWriteAccess.GetHistoryForWrite``1
member.summary: Get write access to an instance of certain history type. It is expected that the caller will filling the contents of the type textures. Null if not requested beforehand. On first get of a type, the type instance is created.
member.returns: True if a type has been requested earlier. False otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryWriteAccess.IsWritten``1
member.summary: Check if a type was already written this frame by some render pass.
member.returns: True if a type has been written earlier. False otherwise.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryReadAccess
member.summary:
User API to get history read access for a user history type instance. Read access is valid and available after the history type has been requested and written by a render pass. Otherwise a null is returned. Typically called by the history type consumer render pass in the render pipeline.
User API for external systems to register history read access callbacks.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryReadAccess.GetHistoryForRead``1
member.summary: Get read access to an instance of certain history type. Available only if the type instance has been requested and written earlier.
member.returns: A class instance of Type. Null if not available on this frame.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryReadAccess.HistoryRequestDelegate
member.summary: Callback type for requesting various history type instances for read. Typically used by systems external to the pipeline. For example: A MonoBehavior requesting access for MonoBehavior.LateUpdate() call.

member.name: E:UnityEngine.Rendering.ICameraHistoryReadAccess.OnGatherHistoryRequests

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem
member.summary:
A convenience base class for camera history items/types. It is recommended to derive from this class to make new history item type.
The owning camera BufferedRTHandleSystem reference is used for central storage. The central storage allows the camera to track all of the history types in a single place. And gives the deriving type a direct access to texture allocation services. Type id is used to deconflict RTHandle ids from different types.
The user is responsible for designing the derived type to work well with the producing and consuming render passes. For example: Add the necessary cpu-side tracking data and update logic. Add methods for accessing the history data and design a suitable API for the type. Handle allocation and deallocation of the history texture RTHandles etc.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.OnCreate(UnityEngine.Rendering.BufferedRTHandleSystem,System.UInt32)
member.summary:
Called internally when a CameraHistoryItem type is created to initialize the RTHandle storage and type id.
User types can override to do additional initialization, such as creating the ids for multiple history RTHandles. Deriving type should call the base.OnCreate() to correctly initialize the CameraHistoryItem first.
member.param: Unique id given to this class type by the owning camera.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.storage
member.summary: The owning camera RTHandle storage for the history textures.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.MakeId(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Creates unique ids for the RTHandle storage. Index == 0, returns the TypeId of this CameraHistoryItem. Index == N, generates new ids in case the user wants to store multiple history textures in the same CameraHistoryItem.
member.param: Index of the type RTHandle, a type local Enum or a user id.
member.returns: A unique id for each type, index and camera.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.AllocHistoryFrameRT(System.Int32,System.Int32,UnityEngine.RenderTextureDescriptor@,System.String)
member.summary: Allocate a history frame RTHandle[] using a descriptor.
member.param: Id for the history RTHandle storage.
Number of RTHandles allocated for the id.
Texture descriptor used for each RTHandle in the allocation.
User visible debug name of the texture.
member.returns: Current frame RTHandle in the allocation.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.AllocHistoryFrameRT(System.Int32,System.Int32,UnityEngine.RenderTextureDescriptor@,UnityEngine.FilterMode,System.String)
member.summary: Allocate a history frame RTHandle[] using a descriptor.
member.param: Id for the history RTHandle storage.
Number of RTHandles allocated for the id.
Texture descriptor used for each RTHandle in the allocation.
User visible debug name of the texture.
member.returns: Current frame RTHandle in the allocation.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.ReleaseHistoryFrameRT(System.Int32)
member.summary: Release the RTHandles allocated for the id.

member.summary: Returns the id RTHandle from the previous frame.

member.returns: The RTHandle from previous frame.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CameraHistoryItem.GetCurrentFrameRT(System.Int32)

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.RenderPipelineGlobalSettingsUtils.TryEnsure``2(``0@,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Checks that a asset exists. If the asset isn't valid, Unity tries the following in order: 1. Loads the asset at the default path. 2. Finds any asset in the project with the same type. 3. If `canCreateNewAsset` is true, creates a new asset in the default path. If Unity finds or creates a valid asset, Unity updates the with it. Otherwise Unity will unregister the settings for the given pipeline.
member.param: The current instance of the asset.
If set to `true`, Unity creates a new asset if it can't find an existing asset in the project.
member.typeparam: The type of global settings asset to check.
The type of `RenderPipeline` that this asset belongs to.
member.returns: The asset that Unity found or created, or `null` if Unity can't find or create a valid asset.

member.summary: Render graph command buffer types inherit from this base class. It provides some shared functionality for all command buffer types.

member.summary: The instance of Unity's CommandBuffer that this class encapsulates, providing access to lower-level rendering commands.

member.summary: See (https://docs.unity3d.com/ScriptReference/Rendering.CommandBuffer-sizeInBytes.html)

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.BaseCommandBuffer.ThrowIfGlobalStateNotAllowed
member.summary: Checks if modifying the global state is permitted by the currently executing render graph pass. If such modifications are not allowed, an InvalidOperationException is thrown.
member.exception: Thrown if the current render graph pass does not permit modifications to global state.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.BaseCommandBuffer.ThrowIfRasterNotAllowed
member.summary: Checks if the Raster Command Buffer has set a valid render target.
member.exception: Thrown if the there are no active render targets.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.BaseCommandBuffer.ValidateTextureHandle(UnityEngine.Rendering.RenderGraphModule.TextureHandle@)
member.summary: Ensures that the texture handle being used is valid for the currently executing render graph pass. This includes checks to ensure that the texture handle is registered for read or write access and is not being used incorrectly as a render target attachment.
member.param: The TextureHandle to validate for the current pass.
member.exception: Throws an exception if the texture handle is not properly registered for the pass or being used incorrectly.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.BaseCommandBuffer.ValidateTextureHandleRead(UnityEngine.Rendering.RenderGraphModule.TextureHandle@)
member.summary: Validates that the specified texture handle is registered for read access within the context of the current executing render graph pass. Throws an exception if the texture is not registered for reading or is used incorrectly as a render target attachment.
member.param: The TextureHandle to validate for read access.
member.exception: Throws an exception if the texture handle is either not registered as a readable resource or misused as both an attachment and a regular texture.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.BaseCommandBuffer.ValidateTextureHandleWrite(UnityEngine.Rendering.RenderGraphModule.TextureHandle@)
member.summary: Validates that the specified texture handle is registered for write access within the context of the current executing render graph pass. Additionally, it checks that built-in textures are not being written to, and that the texture is not incorrectly used as a render target attachment. An exception is thrown if any of these checks fail.
member.param: The TextureHandle to validate for write access.
member.exception: Throws an exception if the texture handle is not registered for writing, attempts to write to a built-in texture, or is misused as both a writeable resource and a render target attachment.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreMatrixUtils
member.summary: Set of utility functions for the Core Scriptable Render Pipeline Library related to Matrix operations

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreMatrixUtils.MatrixTimesTranslation(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4@,UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: This function provides the equivalent of multiplying matrix parameter inOutMatrix with a translation matrix defined by the parameter translation. The order of the equivalent multiplication is inOutMatrix * translation.
member.param: Translation component to multiply to the matrix.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreMatrixUtils.TranslationTimesMatrix(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4@,UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: This function provides the equivalent of multiplying a translation matrix defined by the parameter translation with the matrix specified by the parameter inOutMatrix. The order of the equivalent multiplication is translation * inOutMatrix.
member.param: Translation component to multiply to the matrix.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreMatrixUtils.MultiplyPerspectiveMatrix(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,UnityEngine.Matrix4x4)
member.summary: Multiplies a matrix with a perspective matrix. This function is faster than performing the full matrix multiplication. The operation order is perspective * rhs.
member.param: The perspective matrix to multiply with rhs.
A matrix to be multiply to perspective.
member.returns: Returns the matrix that is the result of the multiplication.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreMatrixUtils.MultiplyOrthoMatrix(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Multiplies a matrix with an orthographic matrix. This function is faster than performing the full matrix multiplication. The operation order is ortho * rhs.
member.param: The ortho matrix to multiply with rhs.
A matrix to be multiply to perspective.
If true, it means that right and left are equivalently distant from center and similarly top/bottom are equivalently distant from center.
member.returns: Returns the matrix that is the result of the multiplication.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreMatrixUtils.MultiplyProjectionMatrix(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Multiplies a matrix with a projection matrix. This function is faster than performing the full matrix multiplication. The operation order is projMatrix * rhs.
member.param: The projection matrix to multiply with rhs.
A matrix to be multiply to perspective.
If true, the projection matrix is a centered ( right+left == top+bottom == 0) orthographic projection, otherwise it is a perspective matrix..
member.returns: Returns the matrix that is the result of the multiplication.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.IDebugData
member.summary: Implementing this interface enables integration with Unity's Rendering debugger, providing a way to manage and control the state of debug data.
member.remarks: Use the `IDebugData` interface to register custom debug data. You can reset the data when necessary, which makes it suitable for debugging scenarios where you need to clear or reset specific data. For example, when the application state changes or during gameplay session resets, or when the **Reset** button is selected in the **Rendering Debugger** window in the Editor or at runtime.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.IDebugData.GetReset
member.summary: Provides the reset callback for resetting the debug data.
member.returns: The reset callback

member.summary: Allocate a new fixed sized RTHandle with the default RTHandle System.
member.param: Number of slices of the RTHandle.
Bit depths of a depth buffer.
Set to true to enable UAV random read writes on the texture.
Set to true if the texture should have mipmaps.
Set to true to automatically generate mipmaps.
Set to true if the depth buffer should be used as a shadow map.
Bias applied to mipmaps during filtering.
Number of MSAA samples for the RTHandle.
Set to true if the texture needs to be bound as a multisampled texture in the shader.
Use this property to set the render texture memoryless modes.
Special treatment of the VR eye texture used in stereoscopic rendering.
member.returns: A new RTHandle.

member.name: F:UnityEngine.Rendering.Volume.sharedProfile
member.summary: The shared Profile that this Volume uses. Modifying sharedProfile changes every Volumes that uses this Profile and also changes the Profile settings stored in the Project.
member.remarks: You should not modify Profiles that sharedProfile returns. If you want to modify the Profile of a Volume, use instead.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Rendering.Volume.profile
member.summary: Gets the first instantiated assigned to the Volume. Modifying profile changes the Profile for this Volume only. If another Volume uses the same Profile, this clones the shared Profile and starts using it from now on.
member.remarks: This property automatically instantiates the Profile and make it unique to this Volume so you can safely edit it via scripting at runtime without changing the original Asset in the Project. Note that if you pass your own Profile, you must destroy it when you finish using it.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.Volume.UpdateColliders
member.summary: Updates the cached list of colliders stored in the Volume.
member.remarks: The Volume class caches a list of colliders for performance and quick access. If you add or remove colliders at runtime, call this method to refresh the cached collider list.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Rendering.VolumeStack
member.summary: Represents the state of a Volume blending update within the Volume system.
member.remarks:
This class is responsible for storing the blending of Volume components across multiple scenes and cameras, By default, a global volume stack is provided by the to handle the blending of Volume data across your project. This global stack simplifies managing and blending volume data at a project-wide level. However, if you require more granular control over the blending process or want to store and manage the blending results separately (e.g., per camera or scene), you can create custom volume stacks using . The blending of volumes is based on a combination of several factors: - **Volume Weight:** Determines how strongly a particular volume influences the final result. - **Volume Parameters:** These can be visual settings such as post-processing effects, lighting adjustments, or other specialized effects defined in the Volume components. - **Camera Volume Stack:** Volume blending can vary per camera, allowing different volumes to be blended for different camera views or scenes.
While the default global volume stack works for most use cases, custom stacks provide greater flexibility and control, allowing developers to manage and store volume blending results at a per-scene or per-camera level. This can be particularly useful in complex rendering setups or when you want to apply different volume blends for different gameplay contexts or visual effects.
Keep in mind that frequent updates to the volume blending process (e.g., every frame) may have an impact on performance, especially when dealing with large numbers of volumes or complex blending operations.

member.returns: The current state of the of type in the stack.

member.returns: The current state of the of the specified type, or null if the type is invalid.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Rendering.CoreUtils.PopulateNullFieldsFrom``1(``0,``0)
member.summary: Populates null fields or collection elements in a target object from a source object of the same type.
member.typeparam: The type of the objects. This must be a reference type (`class`).
member.param: The source object from which to copy field values or collection elements. This cannot be null.
The target object to populate with values from the source object. This cannot be null.
member.remarks:
This method copies non-null field values or collection elements from the source object to the target object. Both objects must be of the same type, and derived or base types are not allowed. Fields are updated only if they are null in the target. If a field is a collection implementing `IList`, the method attempts to copy elements that are null in the target collection.
**Type restrictions**: - `T` must be a reference type. - `source` and `target` must be of the exact same type, not derived or base types. - Collections must implement `IList` and have the same length in both source and target for element-by-element copying.

member.param: True to enable visibility mesh passes, false to disable them.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.GetUseVisibilityMesh
member.summary: Returned value used by the render pipeline to use all visibility mesh passes.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.SetMirrorViewMode(System.Int32)
member.summary: Used to communicate to the XR device how to render the XR MirrorView. Note: not all blit modes are supported by all providers. Blitmode set here serves as preference purpose.
member.param: Mirror view mode to be set as preferred. See `XRMirrorViewBlitMode` for the builtin blit modes.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.GetMirrorViewMode
member.summary: Get current blit modes preferred by XRDisplay

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.SetRenderScale(System.Single)
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to scale the render target on the XR device.
member.param: A value of 1.0f represents 100% of the original resolution.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.GetRenderViewportScale
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to retrieve the applied renderViewportScale value from the XR display. One use case for retriving this value is that render pipeline can properly sync some SRP owned textures to scale accordingly
member.returns: Returns current appliedViewportScale value from the XRDisplaySubsystem.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.GetDynamicResolutionScale
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to retrieve the DynamicResolutionScale value from the XR display. One use case for retrieving this value is that render pipeline can properly sync some SRP owned textures to scale accordingly
member.returns: Returns current DynamicResolutionScale value from the XRDisplaySubsystem.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.ScaleTextureWidthForXR(UnityEngine.RenderTexture)
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to calculate texture scaled width for XR display if it supports dynamic resolution
member.param: Input texture that supports dynamic resolution
member.returns: Returns current scaled width of the input texture.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.ScaleTextureHeightForXR(UnityEngine.RenderTexture)
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to calculate texture scaled height for XR display if it supports dynamic resolution
member.param: Input texture that supports dynamic resolution
member.returns: Returns current scaled width of the input texture.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.NewLayout
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to initiate a new rendering frame through a XR layout.
member.returns: Returns a new default layout.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.EndLayout
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to complete the XR layout at the end of the frame.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRSystem.RenderMirrorView(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Used by the render pipeline to render the mirror view to the gameview, as configured by the XR device.

Camera that has XR device connected to. The connected XR device determines how to perform the mirror view draw.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRBuiltinShaderConstants.UpdateBuiltinShaderConstants(UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,UnityEngine.Matrix4x4,System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Update the shader constant data used by the C++ builtin renderer.
member.param: The new view matrix that XR shaders constant should update to use.
The new projection matrix that XR shaders constant should update to use.
Determines the yflip state for the projection matrix.
Index of the XR shader constant to update.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRBuiltinShaderConstants.SetBuiltinShaderConstants(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer)
member.summary: Bind the shader constants used by the C++ builtin renderer via a command buffer. `UpdateBuiltinShaderConstants` should be called before to update the constants. This is required to maintain compatibility with legacy code and shaders.
member.param: Commandbuffer on which to set XR shader constants.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRBuiltinShaderConstants.SetBuiltinShaderConstants(UnityEngine.Rendering.RasterCommandBuffer)
member.summary: Bind the shader constants used by the C++ builtin renderer via a raster command buffer. `UpdateBuiltinShaderConstants` should be called before to update the constants. This is required to maintain compatibility with legacy code and shaders.
member.param: RasterCommandbuffer on which to set XR shader constants.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRBuiltinShaderConstants.Update(UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass,UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Update and bind shader constants used by the C++ builtin renderer given the XRPass. For better control of setting up builtin shader constants, see `UpdateBuiltinShaderConstants` and `SetBuiltinShaderConstants` which do the same logic but could take in custom projection and view matricies instead. This is required to maintain compatibility with legacy code and shaders.
member.param: The new XRPass that XR shader constants should update to use.
CommandBuffer on which to set XR shader constants.
Determines the yflip state for the projection matrix.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPassCreateInfo
member.summary: Set of data used to create a XRPass object.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass
member.summary: XRPass holds the render target information and a list of XRView. XRView contains the parameters required to render (projection and view matrices, viewport, etc) When a pass has 2 views or more, single-pass will be active if the platform supports it. To avoid allocating every frame, XRView is a struct and XRPass is pooled.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.#ctor
member.summary: Parameterless constructor. Note: in order to avoid GC, the render pipeline should use XRPass.Create instead of this method.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.CreateDefault(UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPassCreateInfo)
member.summary: Default allocator method for XRPass.
member.param: A descriptor used to create and initialize the XRPass.
member.returns: Default XRPass created from createInfo descriptor.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.Release
member.summary: Default release method. Can be overridden by render pipelines.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.enabled
member.summary: Returns true if the pass contains at least one view.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.supportsFoveatedRendering
member.summary: Returns true if the pass can use foveated rendering commands.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.copyDepth
member.summary: If true, the render pipeline is expected to output a valid depth buffer to the renderTarget.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.hasMotionVectorPass
member.summary: If true, the render pipeline is expected to generate motion data and output to the motionVectorRenderTarget.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.spaceWarpRightHandedNDC
member.summary: Reports which NDC convention the render pipeline should use when calculating motion vectors. if true, motion vector data must use the right-handed NDC space. If false motion vector data must use the left-handed NDC space.
member.remarks:
The render pipeline must write motion vector data to the .
> [!NOTE] > The OpenXR specification doesn't specify which coordinate space convention to use for the > motion vector data. Unity only supports SpaceWarp when using the Vulkan graphics API, which uses the right-handed convention for normalized device coordinates, but > devices still can choose either convention for motion data when the > application is using the Vulkan graphics API.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.isFirstCameraPass
member.summary: If true, is the first pass of a xr camera

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.isLastCameraPass
member.summary: If true, is the last pass of a xr camera

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.multipassId
member.summary: Index of the pass inside the frame.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.cullingPassId

member.summary: Queue up render commands to enable single-pass techniques. Note: depending on the platform and settings, either single-pass instancing or the multiview extension will be used.

member.summary: Queue up render commands to disable single-pass techniques.

member.summary: Queue up render commands to disable single-pass techniques.

member.summary: Queue up render commands to disable single-pass techniques.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.hasValidOcclusionMesh
member.summary: Returns true if the pass was setup with expected mesh and material.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.hasValidVisibleMesh
member.summary: Returns true if the pass was setup with expected mesh and enabled by settings.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.RenderOcclusionMesh(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generate commands to render the occlusion mesh for this pass. In single-pass mode : the meshes for all views are combined into one mesh, where the corresponding view index is encoded into each vertex. The keyword "XR_OCCLUSION_MESH_COMBINED" is also enabled when rendering the combined mesh.
member.param: Set to true when rendering into a render texture. Used for handling Unity yflip.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.RenderOcclusionMesh(UnityEngine.Rendering.RasterCommandBuffer,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generate commands to render the occlusion mesh for this pass. In single-pass mode : the meshes for all views are combined into one mesh, where the corresponding view index is encoded into each vertex. The keyword "XR_OCCLUSION_MESH_COMBINED" is also enabled when rendering the combined mesh.
member.param: Set to true when rendering into a render texture. Used for handling Unity yflip.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.RenderVisibleMeshCustomMaterial(UnityEngine.Rendering.RasterCommandBuffer,System.Single,UnityEngine.Material,UnityEngine.MaterialPropertyBlock,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generate commands to render the visible mesh for this pass using a custom material and set of material property block. In single-pass mode : the meshes for all views are combined into one mesh, where the corresponding view index is encoded into each vertex.
member.param: Material that the visibility mesh will render.
Material block with all the shader parameters that need to be set.
Material shader pass to render, set 0 by default.
Set to true when rendering into a render texture. Used for handling Unity yflip.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.RenderVisibleMeshCustomMaterial(UnityEngine.Rendering.CommandBuffer,System.Single,UnityEngine.Material,UnityEngine.MaterialPropertyBlock,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Generate commands to render the visible mesh for this pass using a custom material and set of material property block. In single-pass mode : the meshes for all views are combined into one mesh, where the corresponding view index is encoded into each vertex.
member.param: Material that the visibility mesh will render.
Material block with all the shader parameters that need to be set.
Material shader pass to render, set 0 by default.
Set to true when rendering into a render texture. Used for handling Unity yflip.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.RenderDebugXRViewsFrustum
member.summary: Draw debug line for all XR views.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.ApplyXRViewCenterOffset(UnityEngine.Vector2)
member.summary: Take a point that is center-relative (0.5, 0.5) and modify it to be placed relative to the view's center instead, respecting the asymmetric FOV (if it is used)
member.param: Center relative point for symmetric FOV.
member.returns: View center relative points. First view center is stored in x,y components and second view center is stored in z,w components.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPass.InitBase(UnityEngine.Experimental.Rendering.XRPassCreateInfo)
member.summary: Initialize the base class fields.
member.param: A descriptor used to create and initialize the XRPass.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.LightAnchor
member.summary: Represents camera-space light controls around a virtual pivot point.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.yaw
member.summary: The camera-relative yaw.
member.remarks: The range is -180 through 180 inclusive. Values between 0 and 180 are to the right of the camera, and values between 0 and -180 to the left.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.pitch
member.summary: The pitch relative to the horizon or camera depending on value of m_Space.
member.remarks: The range is -180 through 180 inclusive. Values between 0 and 180 are below the camera, and values between 0 and -180 are above the camera.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.roll
member.summary: The camera-relative roll.
member.remarks: The range is -180 through 180 inclusive. Values between 0 and 180 are to the right of the camera, and values between 0 and -180 are to the left of the camera.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.distance
member.summary: The distance from the light's anchor point.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.UpDirection
member.summary: Enum to describes to up vector for the Light Anchor

member.name: F:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.UpDirection.World
member.summary: Up vector is world space Vector3.up

member.name: F:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.UpDirection.Local
member.summary: Up vector is the up of the main camera

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.frameSpace
member.summary: Indicates whether the up vector should be in world or camera space.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.anchorPosition
member.summary: The position of the light's anchor point.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.anchorPositionOverride
member.summary: Overrides the pivot of used to compute the light position. This is useful to track an existing object in the scene. The transform of the light will be automatically updated by the Update() method of the LightAnchor.

member.name: P:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.anchorPositionOffset
member.summary: Offset relative to the position of the anchor position override transform in object space.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.NormalizeAngleDegree(System.Single)
member.summary: Normalizes the input angle to be in the range of -180 and 180.
member.returns: Returns the angle of rotation between -180 and 180.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.SynchronizeOnTransform(UnityEngine.Camera)
member.summary: Updates Yaw, Pitch, Roll, and Distance based on the Transform.
member.param: The Camera to which light values are relative.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.LightAnchor.UpdateTransform(UnityEngine.Camera,UnityEngine.Vector3)
member.summary: Updates the light's transform with respect to a given camera and anchor point
member.param: The camera to which values are relative.
